_notes: Phase AT — chip catalog for the explicit artifact-type picker. Rendered as turn-1 chips in AIHarness when the wizard cannot confidently auto-classify the user's goal. Intentionally short (7 chips) to fit on small windows in 2 rows max. The 'other' chip routes into a free-text follow-up. Order = display order in the UI.

chips.label: Web mini-app
chips.subtitle: HTML/JS in a viewer or browser
chips.examples: habit tracker, drawing tool, simple game

chips.label: macOS app
chips.subtitle: Native Swift, runs in its own window
chips.examples: menubar utility, document editor, native game

chips.label: AI Pipeline
chips.subtitle: Chain of skill steps that produce a result
chips.examples: summarize the news, translate a book, generate a report

chips.label: AI Agent
chips.subtitle: An LLM that uses tools to accomplish a task
chips.examples: research assistant, code reviewer, task triager

chips.label: Unity game
chips.subtitle: C# project, opens in Unity Hub

chips.label: Other / not sure
chips.subtitle: Tell me more — I'll figure it out
chips.examples: answer a question, do a one-shot task, something else

_routing_notes: fences_to_solution_type maps the chip ID onto the AISolutionType enum value the wizard uses internally (AIHarness.swift). 'other' is special — taps open a free-text follow-up message that re-runs the LLM classifier with the extra context, then either picks one of the 6 concrete chips or flips the session into 'answer mode' (Layer 1 = answer-not-build).
